person.name: Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths

nameForms.fullText: Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
"In 1299 Henry son of Richard de Worsley granted to Robert his son lands in Worsley called Mokenis, the bounds beginning at Acornsyke, where it was met by the fall of Kronkysker, between Worsley and Astley; along the fall to Blackbrook, thence by the bounds of Astley and Irlam, across the moss to Ringand Pits, and thence going down to the Meadowyard."

On 25 July 1301: "At York, on the Morrow of St. John the Baptist, 29 Edward I - between Robert, son of Henry de Workesleye, plaintiff, by Robert de Asshton, his guardian, put in his place, and Henry de Workesleye, deforciant of 100 acres of wood and 200 acres of pasture in Workesleye (Worsley). Henry acknowledged the tenement to be the right of Robert, son of Henry, and rendered it to him, to hold to him and his heirs in perpetuity, of the chief lords of the fee, by the services thereto belonging. For this acknowledgment, Robert, son of Henry, gave him 40 lbs. sterling."

Robert also received, from his father, five hundred acres of wood and five hundred acres of pasture, called the Boothes, and from him descended the Worsleys of Boothes, also in Worsley township.

In 1322, Margaret, formerly wife of Henry, sold and released to Robert her son all her goods in Worsley, movable and immovable, for £40 sterling which he had paid her. 

He had a share of the manor, known as Booths, assigned to him in 1323, so that in future, out of the free rent, he and his heirs were to pay 2s. to the chief lord, leaving 18s. to be paid by the lord of Worsley.

It's known he died before 1350, as in that year Agnes, widow of Robert de Worsley, claimed her dower in twenty-one messuages and various lands in Worsley and Heaton Norris. William son of Robert, in defending, denied Agnes's marriage, but she averred that it took place on the Wednesday after 29 Aug. 1346, at the door of St. Mary's Church, Deane; De Banco R. 363, m. 78 d. 

Robert Worseley of Boothes or Bothes in Worseley, co. Lancs, was a younger son of:

Henry Worseley of Worseley & Hilton, son of Richard Worseley.
and Margaret [-- of Shorisworth ____].
Siblings
Richard of Worseley.

«b»Marriage and Children«/b»
Robert married Cecilia de Bromhall and they had the following children:

1.) William, m. Maud, dau. of John Hulton of Farnworth.

2.) John

3.) Ellen, m. Thomlyn Booth of Barton.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths於大約1294年出生在英國時，他的父親Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsley55歲，母親Margaret de Shoresworth26歲。他於1318年之前與Cicely Bromhall在英國藍卡爾克雷頓 勒 伍茲結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1350年之前在他的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Worsley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Worsley in Lancashire or Worsley Farm in Rock (Worcestershire). The Lancashire placename may derive from an Old English personal name Weorcgȳth (genitive Weorcgȳthes) + Old English lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. The Worcestershire placename may take its name from a lost stream-name Worf + Old English lēah.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths was born about 1294, in England, his father, Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsley, was 55 and his mother, Margaret de Shoresworth, was 26. He married Cicely Bromhall before 1318, in Clayton Le Woods, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died before 1350, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths est né environ 1294, à Angleterre, son père, Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsley, avait 55 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Shoresworth, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Cicely Bromhall avant 1318, à Clayton Le Woods, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé avant 1350, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths um 1294 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsley 55 und seine Mutter Margaret de Shoresworth 26. Er hat vor 1318 in Clayton Le Woods, Lancashire, England Cicely Bromhall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb vor 1350 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths è nato il circa 1294 nel England, il suo padre, Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsley, aveva 55 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Shoresworth, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Cicely Bromhall prima del 1318, nel Clayton Le Woods, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì prima del 1350, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Worsley Lord of Boothsが約1294年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsleyは55歳，母Margaret de Shoresworthは26歳であった。1318年以前にイギリスランカシュアイアクレイトン ル ウッドスでCicely Bromhallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1350年以前に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths(이)가 대략 1294년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsley(은)는 55세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Shoresworth(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1318년 이전 영국 랭카실르 클레이턴 일 우드즈에서 Cicely Bromhall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1350년 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths nasceu aproximadamente 1294, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsley, tinha 55 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Shoresworth, tinha 26 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Worsley Lord of Booths родился около 1294 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Worsley 4th Lord of Worsley было 55 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Shoresworth, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Cicely Bromhall перед 1318 г. в местности клеитон л вудс, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер перед 1350 г. в его родном городе.
